message:
docs(fnp-conformance): add fixtures/PROVENANCE.md covering all 45 fixtures (br-franken_numpy-pqam)

Per the testing-conformance-harnesses skill, every fixture must record
generator, reference NumPy version, command, and last regeneration date
so divergences at regen time can be diagnosed.

The previous COVERAGE.md provenance table covered 6 of 45 fixture files.
This new file covers all 45 top-level fixtures plus 14 packet-scoped and
oracle-output subdirectory artifacts. Each row names its generator
(capture_numpy_oracle / manual / programmatic), the NumPy version used
(2.4.3 for captured fixtures), the command that regenerates it, and the
last-regen date (from git log for existing fixtures).

Adds the regeneration and upgrade protocol so a future NumPy bump has a
documented workflow: bump reference version, re-capture oracle fixtures,
diff-review, and either accept the diff or file a DISCREPANCIES.md entry.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,134 @@
+# Fixture Provenance
+
+> Mandatory per the testing-conformance-harnesses skill. Every fixture records
+> *how* it was generated: tool, NumPy version, command, and last regeneration
+> date. When a regenerated fixture disagrees with the committed copy, this
+> table is the first place to look.
+
+## Environment baseline
+
+- **Reference NumPy:** 2.4.3 (installed at `/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/numpy`)
+- **Reference Python:** 3.13.7
+- **Oracle capture binary:** `cargo run --bin capture_numpy_oracle -p fnp-conformance`
+- **Oracle Python resolver:** honors `$FNP_ORACLE_PYTHON`; otherwise probes `python3` on `$PATH`
+- **Pure-Python fallback:** used when no NumPy interpreter is available; gate with `FNP_REQUIRE_REAL_NUMPY_ORACLE=1` to forbid fallback in CI
+- **Repository root at last regen:** `git rev-parse HEAD` at each fixture's last-regen date below
+- **Fixture ID policy:** IDs must be snake_case lowercase; enforced by `validate_fixture_id`
+
+## Generator key
+
+- `capture_numpy_oracle` — runs `capture_numpy_oracle` on the matching `*_input_cases.json` or directly against live NumPy; writes a normalized `cases[]` record per input
+- `manual` — hand-authored cases, typically tabulated adversarial / boundary / IEEE-754 scenarios that do not come from a live oracle
+- `programmatic` — generated by a Rust test helper or a one-off script in `src/` that enumerates a cartesian product (e.g. dtype × dtype) and records expected values
+- `oracle_capture_script` — a specific generator function inside `src/<domain>.rs`; see referenced lines
+
+## Fixture table (45 JSON files under `fixtures/`)
+
+| File | Generator | NumPy | Command | Last regen | Notes |
+|------|-----------|:-----:|---------|:----------:|-------|

+| `ufunc_metamorphic_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written invariant laws | 2026-04-20 | commutativity, linearity |
+| `ufunc_adversarial_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written edge/error cases | 2026-04-18 | NaN/Inf propagation, overflow, invalid axis |
+| `dtype_promotion_cases.json` | programmatic | 2.4.3 | `np.result_type` enumerated over 14×14 type matrix | 2026-04-18 | 198 cases, full matrix |
+| `shape_stride_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written stride-tricks & broadcast scenarios | 2026-04-18 | 0-D, empty, negative-stride, 5D/6D, F-order |
+| `shape_stride_metamorphic_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written invariants | 2026-04-18 | reshape roundtrip, transpose identity, broadcast commute |
+| `shape_stride_adversarial_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written error cases | 2026-04-18 | incompatible broadcast, negative-infer reshape |
+| `runtime_policy_cases.json` | manual | — | CompatibilityClass enumeration | 2026-04-18 | risk thresholds at 0.0, 0.69, 0.7, 0.71, 1.0 |
+| `runtime_policy_metamorphic_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written invariants | 2026-04-18 | monotonicity, subsumption, reflexive |
+| `runtime_policy_adversarial_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written hostile payloads | 2026-04-18 | malformed / unknown wire-class |
+| `override_audit_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written audit events | 2026-04-18 | override event log shape checks |
+| `iter_differential_cases.json` | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | packet FNP-P2C-004 replay | 2026-04-18 | selector/overlap/flag/flatiter parity |
+| `iter_metamorphic_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written invariants | 2026-04-18 | repeatability/idempotence/count laws |
+| `iter_adversarial_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written hostile payloads | 2026-04-18 | stable reason-code failures |
+| `rng_differential_cases.json` | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | packet FNP-P2C-007 witness capture | 2026-04-20 | PCG64 witness corpus |
+| `rng_distribution_differential_cases.json` | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | distribution-draw capture | 2026-04-18 | currently XFAIL — seeding mismatch with NumPy PCG64 SeedSequence |
+| `rng_metamorphic_cases.json` | manual | — | hand-written invariants | 2026-04-20 | jump additivity, fill/iter equivalence, bounded repeatability |

+

+| `packet002_dtype/dtype_differential_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-002 | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | 2026-04-18 |
+| `packet002_dtype/dtype_metamorphic_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-002 | manual | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `packet002_dtype/dtype_adversarial_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-002 | manual | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `packet003_transfer/iter_differential_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-003 | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | 2026-03-18 |
+| `packet003_transfer/iter_metamorphic_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-003 | manual | — | 2026-03-18 |
+| `packet003_transfer/iter_adversarial_cases.json` | FNP-P2C-003 | manual | — | 2026-03-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/ufunc_oracle_output.json` | — | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | 2026-04-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/ufunc_differential_report.json` | — | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/iter_differential_report.json` | FNP-P2C-004 | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/io_differential_report.json` | FNP-P2C-009 | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/linalg_differential_report.json` | FNP-P2C-008 | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `oracle_outputs/datetime_differential_report.json` | — | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `packet002_dtype/oracle_outputs/dtype_differential_report.json` | FNP-P2C-002 | comparator binary | — | 2026-04-18 |
+| `packet003_transfer/oracle_outputs/iter_differential_report.json` | FNP-P2C-003 | comparator binary | — | 2026-03-18 |
+
+## Regeneration workflow
+
+For `capture_numpy_oracle` fixtures:
+
+```bash
+export FNP_ORACLE_PYTHON=/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/python3  # NumPy-backed
+export FNP_REQUIRE_REAL_NUMPY_ORACLE=1                    # no silent fallback
+cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin capture_numpy_oracle
+# captures ufunc_input_cases.json → oracle_outputs/ufunc_oracle_output.json
+```
+
+For `manual` fixtures: edit the JSON directly, rerun the affected differential
+test, and update `Last regen` in this table.
+
+For `programmatic` fixtures (dtype_promotion_cases.json): rerun the helper
+referenced in its `generator` field. Record the exact command in this file.
+
+## Upgrade protocol
+
+When upgrading the reference NumPy version (e.g. 2.4.3 → 2.5.x):
+
+1. Update the `Reference NumPy` line at the top of this file.
+2. Run `cargo run -p fnp-conformance --bin capture_numpy_oracle` for every
+   `capture_numpy_oracle` fixture listed above.
+3. `git diff fixtures/` — review every changed row. File a bead for each
+   divergence that is *not* explained by the NumPy changelog.
+4. Update each affected fixture's `Last regen` row in this file.
+5. If a divergence is an intentional NumPy-side bugfix that we should match,
+   the diff is accepted — bump the `NumPy` column. If it is an intentional
+   fnp divergence, document it in `../DISCREPANCIES.md` with a new `DISC-NNN`
+   ID and set the test to XFAIL.
+
+## Gaps
+
+- `capture_numpy_oracle` binary currently only regenerates `ufunc_input_cases`.
+  Extending it to regenerate the other `*_differential_cases.json` files is
+  tracked by bead `franken_numpy-gghj` (`[MEDIUM] fnp-conformance single-command
+  fixture regeneration workflow missing`).
+- Fixtures generated before 2026-03-12 (`rng_statistical_cases.json`) predate
+  this provenance scheme; their NumPy column is `—` because no record exists.
+  Regenerating them is safe but not currently scheduled.
